narration: A palindrome uses inward traversal because the useful clues live at both ends of the word.
callout: Compare the first and last letters before moving inward.
decision: If both letters match, keep going.

narration: Each matching pair lets you squeeze the search area smaller.
callout: After matching r with r, move to the next mirrored pair.

predictionPrompt.prompt: What should happen if the letters do not match?

choices.label: Return false right away
choices.feedback: Correct. One mismatch proves the word is not a palindrome.

choices.label: Keep checking the rest
choices.feedback: Not this one. The palindrome rule is already broken.

narration: When all mirrored pairs match, the middle does not need extra work.
callout: The pointers can stop once left is no longer before right.
decision: That means the whole word passed the symmetry check.

reflectionPrompt.prompt: Why can the algorithm stop after the first mismatch?
